question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId string where accountId[0] is a large Long value (>127) so that the boxed Long.valueOf(accountId[0]) == shard comparison uses reference equality and always returns false, causing the monitor to throw an exception or silently skip shard validation, allowing transactions to be routed to a mismatched shard and causing transaction history to be recorded under the wrong shard?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted operator accountId (e.g., '0.0.2' with leading/trailing whitespace or unicode lookalikes) that passes the DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID.equals() check but causes the shard/realm rewrite via operator.setAccountId('%d.%d.%s'.formatted(shard, realm, accountId[2])) to produce a malformed account ID, causing all subsequent transactions to be attributed to a wrong account in the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId with more than three dot-separated segments (e.g., '0.0.2.extra') so that accountId.split('\\\\.', 3) produces a three-element array where accountId[2] contains 'extra' appended, causing the reformatted account ID to be invalid and transactions to be submitted with a corrupted payer account, reorganizing the transaction history attribution?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a value where accountId[0] and accountId[1] are non-numeric strings that pass the split but cause Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException, bypassing the shard/realm check entirely and allowing the monitor to start with an unvalidated operator account, leading to transactions being recorded under an unverified account in the ledger history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user leave the mirrorNode field null (its @Nullable default) so that getMirrorNode() falls back to network::getMirrorNode, and then manipulate the network enum value to OTHER, causing the mirror node host to be constructed as '.mirrornode.hedera.com' (empty prefix), redirecting all transaction history queries to an attacker-controlled DNS entry and allowing the attacker to serve a reordered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Objects.requireNonNullElseGet(this.mirrorNode, network::getMirrorNode) fallback by setting network to OTHER (which constructs a MirrorNodeProperties with host '.mirrornode.hedera.com'), causing the REST API client to connect to an invalid or attacker-controlled mirror node endpoint and receive a falsified transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a custom mirrorNode with a host pointing to an attacker-controlled server (since mirrorNode is @Nullable and @Valid but has no hostname allowlist), causing the monitor to retrieve a fabricated address book from the attacker's mirror node, which then populates the nodes list with attacker-controlled consensus nodes, reorganizing which nodes process and record transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.port to a non-443 value, causing MirrorNodeProperties.RestProperties.getBaseUrl() to use 'http://' instead of 'https://', enabling a network-level attacker to perform a MITM attack on the unencrypted REST connection and inject a falsified transaction history or reordered address book?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.grpc.host to an 'in-process:' prefixed value (matching the bypass check in GrpcProperties.getEndpoint()), causing the gRPC subscriber to connect to an in-process stub rather than the real mirror node, allowing the attacker to inject fabricated transaction history responses?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to exactly DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID ('0.0.2') while setting commonProperties.shard to a different value, causing init() to rewrite the operator accountId to a different shard via operator.setAccountId('%d.%d.%s'.formatted(shard, realm, accountId[2])), so that all transactions are submitted under a cross-shard account ID that maps to a different account in the ledger history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an operator accountId where accountId[2] (the account number segment) contains special format characters (e.g., '%s', '%d') that, when passed to '%d.%d.%s'.formatted(shard, realm, accountId[2]), produce a format injection, causing the resulting account ID string to be malformed and transactions to be attributed to an unintended account in the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a value with an empty third segment (e.g., '0.0.') so that accountId[2] is an empty string, causing operator.setAccountId('%d.%d.%s'.formatted(shard, realm, '')) to produce '0.0.' which is an invalid account ID, causing the monitor to submit transactions with a malformed payer that gets recorded inconsistently in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId of exactly two segments (e.g., '0.0') so that accountId.split('\\\\.', 3) returns a two-element array, causing the accountId.length != 3 check to throw IllegalArgumentException and prevent the monitor from starting, effectively causing a denial-of-service that disrupts transaction history continuity?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network field to HederaNetwork.OTHER while leaving nodes empty, causing NodeSupplier.refresh() to fall through all sources (configured nodes, address book, network.getNodes()) and emit an error, preventing the monitor from submitting transactions and creating gaps in the transaction history that could be exploited to reorder or replay transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @PostConstruct init() method performs no validation of the operator.privateKey field, allowing a default or weak private key (the hardcoded '302e020100...' key in OperatorProperties) to be used in production, enabling an attacker who knows the default key to submit transactions as the operator and inject entries into the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure mirrorNode.restJava to point to an attacker-controlled server while mirrorNode.rest points to the legitimate mirror node, causing RestApiClient to route /network/ path requests (including address book retrieval) to the attacker's server via the webClientRestJava client, allowing the attacker to serve a poisoned address book that redirects transactions to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.restJava to null (its default), causing RestApiClient to use the same webClientRest for both REST and REST Java endpoints, and then set mirrorNode.rest.host to an attacker-controlled server, causing all network node queries (getNodes(), getNetworkStakeStatusCode()) to return attacker-controlled data that reorganizes the node list used for transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network field to HederaNetwork.TESTNET while the system is intended for MAINNET, causing all transactions to be submitted to the testnet ledger instead of mainnet, effectively reorganizing the production transaction history by routing transactions to the wrong network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.grpc.port to 0 (which passes the @Min(0) constraint), causing MirrorNodeProperties.GrpcProperties.getEndpoint() to return 'host:0', connecting the gRPC subscriber to an invalid port and causing it to fall back to an unintended mirror node, which could serve a reordered transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeValidation.enabled to false, causing NodeSupplier.validateNode() to skip all node validation and add every configured node (including attacker-injected ones) to the active node list without verification, allowing attacker-controlled nodes to receive transactions and record them in a manipulated order in the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeValidation.retrieveAddressBook to false, causing NodeSupplier.getAddressBook() to return Flux.empty() and skip the address book fetch, forcing the monitor to use only the statically configured nodes (which may be attacker-controlled), allowing the attacker to control which nodes process transactions and how they appear in the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeValidation.tls to TlsMode.PLAINTEXT, causing NodeSupplier.refresh() to filter out all TLS-enabled nodes (port 50212) via the predicate 'port -> port != TLS_PORT', forcing all transactions to be submitted over unencrypted connections where a network-level attacker can intercept and reorder them before they reach the consensus node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeValidation.maxNodes to 1, causing NodeSupplier.refresh() to take only a single node via .take(monitorProperties.getNodeValidation().getMaxNodes()), concentrating all transaction submissions to a single node and making it trivial for an attacker controlling that node to reorder or delay transactions in the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeValidation.frequency to the minimum Duration.ofSeconds(30), causing NodeSupplier to refresh the node list every 30 seconds, and during each refresh window inject a malicious node into the nodes set that processes transactions out of order before being removed, creating gaps or reorderings in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set nodeValidation.maxEndpointsPerNode to a large value, causing NodeSupplier.toNodeProperties() to include many endpoints per node via .limit(nodeValidation.getMaxEndpointsPerNode()), and inject attacker-controlled service endpoints from a poisoned address book, causing transactions to be routed to attacker-controlled endpoints that record them in a manipulated order?
